disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A small tender red bump on the nape of the neck 4 days ago has rapidly enlarged over the last 48 hours into a large, firm, hot, red painful lump
trigger_question_examples: When did the lump start and how has it changed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started as a little sore bump about 4 days ago, but in the last two days it's ballooned into this big hot red lump.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A yellow-white pus-filled point (pointing head) developed on the centre of the lump yesterday
trigger_question_examples: Is there a head or any pus on the lump?
What does the middle of the lump look like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, since yesterday there's a yellow-white head in the middle of it, like it's about to burst.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Chills and generalised malaise this afternoon in association with the lesion
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fever, chills or shivers?
Do you feel unwell in yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've had chills and shivers all afternoon and I feel generally run-down and unwell.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The redness is extending outward a couple of centimetres beyond the lump
trigger_question_examples: Is the redness staying put or spreading?
Has the red area got bigger than the lump itself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The redness does seem to be creeping out past the lump now, further than it was this morning.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Neck movement is severely restricted, described as mechanical difficulty turning the head because of the lump and pain
trigger_question_examples: Is it the pain stopping you moving, or something else?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can barely turn my head — it's the pain and the size of the lump that stops me, not a headache or anything.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No difficulty swallowing, no drooling, no muffled voice, no difficulty breathing, no jaw-opening restriction
trigger_question_examples: Any trouble swallowing, drooling, or a change in your voice?
Is it hard to open your mouth or breathe?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I can swallow and breathe fine and my voice is normal — it's just the lump and my neck being stiff.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone with no one immediately available to help overnight
trigger_question_examples: Is there anyone at home with you?
Do you have someone who can check on you tonight?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I live on my own and there's no one here with me.
